what_it_does: Declares a generated surface specification for view D4, an idea-capture organ in area D, targeting desktop and mobile, tied to journey J6 and three UI addresses, with build instructions pointing to components, a design lint gate, and a token-to-CSS source.
observations: It is a generated surface-spec, not hand-authored
Home journey is J6 and journeys contains only J6; cross_journeys is empty
Represents the concept INB-idea and status is planned
Addresses span board/idea-capture and two inbox/ paths
build_to names components board and inbox, a lint command, and a single token source
The preamble states addresses must not be invented
standouts: The surface is titled 'Idea-capture organ' and represents INB-idea
Addresses bridge board and inbox namespaces
build_to couples a lint gate with a token-derived CSS pipeline
conventions: Uses directory path as a journey spine
Keeps journeys and cross_journeys as address-ref carriers
References only real addresses, never invented ones
Separates surface metadata from build instructions
concerns: status is planned, so the spec may be unrealized
cross_journeys is empty even though addresses span board and inbox
Addresses are listed but not resolved or validated inside the file
notes: This is a planned desktop/mobile surface that wires the idea-capture concept to board and inbox addresses under journey J6.
questions: What do the three ui:// addresses resolve to in code?
What is the B3 web referenced in _what?
How does the design_lint 'FORM gate' decide failure?
Are the board and inbox components already implemented or also planned?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to specify a generated surface for view D4, and the file does exactly that through metadata, addresses, and build targets, so purpose and actual align.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for visual tokens
Build-time validation via a lint gate
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to formalize a UI surface as a first-class, address-referenced artifact that can be built from a design system and validated against code.
novelty: The 'idea-capture organ' framing, the explicit 'never invent an address' constraint, and the build_to section that couples lint and token generation.
contribution: A concrete surface-to-address mapping for D4, a planned idea-capture view, including its journey affiliation, target platforms, representing concept, and build-time dependencies.
summary_for_embedding: company/design/blueprint/surfaces/J6/D4.json is a generated surface specification for the planned desktop/mobile view D4, an idea-capture organ in area D tied to journey J6 and three board/inbox UI addresses, with build instructions referencing board/inbox components, a design lint gate, and a token-derived CSS source.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to provide a build-ready, address-grounded design substrate for an idea-capture surface spanning board and inbox contexts.
